topic: §4.2.2 c4 — verify ISS-1671 (Domain 11 stale label removal) + ISS-1651 (3.6× arithmetic disambiguation)
our_argument_summary: c3 review (queue 277) flagged H1 (Domain 11 stale label) and EXP-253 c1 left over the H1 (3.6× envelope ambiguity, ISS-1651). Decider self-applied two minor patches: ISS-1671 replaced "Domain 11 (archived 2026-04-07; verified live at john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html on 2026-04-27)" with the neutral attribution "the dome’s live-power dashboard (verified live at https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html on 2026-04-27)"; ISS-1651 replaced the bare "3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope" phrasing with the explicit-arithmetic form "the BOU 2017 baseline of −10.9 nT is roughly 3.6× the rule’s ±3 nT half-width (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome’s own acceptance window." Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds; this c4 attacks only the patched text and the surrounding sentences for regression and surviving holes. Note: queue item 289 will independently re-review the EXP-275 coherence-rule paragraph (separate ISS-1672 fix) — that paragraph is in the live section but is NOT in scope for this c4 review.

ISS_1671_label_check.verified_by: string-presence scan of §4.2.2 slice in data/sections.json
ISS_1671_label_check.neutral_attribution_text: the dome’s live-power dashboard (verified live at john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html on 2026-04-27)
ISS_1671_label_check.verdict: patch successfully closed c3 Hole 0 (stale label). Defender cannot point at "that label doesn’t exist" anymore — the URL+page-name attribution is layout-resilient and source-citable.

ISS_1651_arithmetic_check.arithmetic_check: 10.9 / 3 = 3.6333…; rounding to 3.6 (2 sig figs) is standard.
ISS_1651_arithmetic_check.verdict: patch successfully disambiguates the 3.6× ratio. A reader can no longer mistake it for 3.6× the full ±3 nT interval (which would imply 21.6 nT) — the parenthetical pins it to half-width arithmetic.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — section-level prose review; no monitor.py code involved.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Section quotes a verbatim live.html falsification rule ("Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction).") and computes the 3.6× ratio inline (|−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6).
data_sources_used: BOU 2017 magnetometer datum (−10.9 nT) cited via §4.2.5 internal link
Chapman 1933 / Meza et al. 2021 / two ScienceDirect references for 5–25 nT range
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Paragraph 7 (post-Sq-residual fourth-horn defense) and the EXP-275 coherence-rule paragraph close the two main defender retreats. The patch under review here does not modify those defenses.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the patches under review.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level prose, not a per-WIN code review. The eclipse WIN cluster (WIN-010 etc.) carries the monitoring tags individually.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level argument, not a WIN-level relabel claim.

holes_found.description: PRE-EXISTING (carried from c3 Hole 3, deliberately not addressed by ISS-1671/ISS-1651 patches) — closing parenthetical still reads "well outside the dome’s own acceptance window." The ±3 nT envelope is the dome’s FALSIFICATION rule (rule fires WHEN measurement is within ±3 of 0), so technically the dome ACCEPTS its own prediction OUTSIDE that window. "Acceptance window" is therefore inverted — under the rule, ±3 nT is the dome-falsification / globe-passing region. In context the meaning is still clear (the sentence as a whole correctly says BOU is in the dome-passing region, which is the dome’s own prediction), but the term is internally inconsistent with "falsification half-width" used three clauses earlier in the same sentence. Now compounded: the same sentence uses "falsification rule" → "falsification half-width" → "acceptance window" — three names for two related but different things in one breath.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "acceptance window" with "falsification window" (matching the earlier "falsification half-width") OR with the neutral phrase "±3 nT envelope." Optional polish, not blocking. If touched, also consider de-nesting the parenthetical (see new minor finding below).

holes_found.description: NEW (introduced by ISS-1651 patch): the BOU-trilemma sentence now contains a NESTED parenthetical. The full structure is: "The dome must either accept its own falsification criterion ( and concede the globe prediction it asserts is wrong, since the BOU 2017 baseline of −10.9 nT is roughly 3.6× the rule’s ±3 nT half-width ( |−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6 ), well outside the dome’s own acceptance window ) or revise the criterion (and concede the prediction was never discriminating in the first place)." Two levels of parens with the inner arithmetic interrupting the flow. Readability cost is small but real — the sentence is already a 7-clause trilemma, and the nested paren makes it harder to parse. Defender does not gain a rhetorical foothold here, but a copy-editing pass could improve readability.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-flow as: "…since the BOU 2017 baseline of −10.9 nT is roughly 3.6× the rule’s ±3 nT half-width — i.e., |−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6 — placing it well outside the dome’s own ±3 nT window) or revise the criterion…" Em-dashes for the arithmetic aside, plus the "acceptance/falsification" terminology fix from the prior hole. Optional polish.

holes_found.description: PRE-EXISTING (carried from c3 Hole 2, c2 minor 3 — analyst flagged optional in EXP-267 changes_NOT_made) — TLDR third clause "and one of the dome’s own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT ‘globe’ window" still strips the trilemma framing and asserts BOU-2017-already-falsifies as a stand-alone fact. TLDR-only readers (the design intent of progressive disclosure) still see the bare claim. Defender retreat ("your TLDR commits a category error — the ±3 nT rule applies to the 2026 measurement, not the 2017 baseline") still works on a TLDR-only reader. Not regressed by the c3→c4 patches; flagged for completeness.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. c2 suggested replacement still applies: "…and the dome’s published rule, applied consistently, also flags the dome’s own −10.9 nT BOU-2017 baseline input." Phrasing avoids the bare "sits outside" assertion. Not blocking.

holes_found.description: PRE-EXISTING (carried from c3 Hole 4, c2 minor 4) — c1 stronger_arguments[0] (E001–E009 dome’s own 2026 predictions all sit outside ±3 nT, providing a tighter form of second-horn collapse) was again not incorporated. Analyst marked optional in EXP-267 and EXP-267-followup. Confirmed unchanged; not blocking.

stronger_arguments.description: Half-width vs threshold terminology. "Half-width" is correct mathematically (a ±3 interval has half-width 3) but is more often used in spectroscopy/peak-fitting context. "Threshold" or "tolerance" or "radius" of the ±3 nT window would be more reader-friendly and matches how the dome itself frames the rule ("within ±3 nT of 0" — the 3 IS the threshold). The arithmetic disambiguator (|−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6) carries the meaning regardless, so this is a polish-only suggestion.
sources: no external citation needed — terminology preference

stronger_arguments.description: The ratio 3.6× could be replaced by the absolute exceedance "~7.9 nT past the nearest edge of the ±3 nT window (10.9 − 3 = 7.9)" which more directly answers the implicit question "how far outside?" — though 3.6× is what the original c1 phrasing tried to communicate, so changing the unit type would be a larger rewrite. Not recommended unless the section is being rewritten anyway.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome’s eclipse prediction does have a kernel of structural cleverness — a TWO-baseline (BOU + W004) prediction window plus a ±3 nT falsification rule does in principle constrain the dome’s claim, and writing the rule down on a public site IS more methodologically honest than most dome-cosmology claims that have no falsifier at all. The dome author deserves credit for having a published falsifier.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The published falsifier compares against "globe predicts 0 nT," which §4.2.2 establishes is the wrong globe prediction (Chapman 5–25 nT is what standard physics actually predicts). And the dome’s OWN baseline input (BOU 2017 −10.9 nT) violates the dome’s OWN ±3 nT window before the 2026 measurement is taken — a self-contradiction the patches under review (ISS-1671 + ISS-1651) make sharper, not weaker, by clarifying the arithmetic and removing the layout-fragile label.

reasoning_for_recommendation: Both ISS-1671 and ISS-1651 patches landed cleanly and closed the c3 Hole 0 and the EXP-253 c1 H1 holes. No regressions. The two new+pre-existing minor findings (acceptance/falsification terminology + nested parenthetical readability + c3 minors 2 and 4) are all polish-grade and were ALL flagged in c3 as optional or not-blocking. Filing fresh issues for them would be churn. Surface them in this review for the next holistic-pass operator, then move on. If the operator wants ANY single optional polish picked up, the highest-value one is replacing "acceptance window" with "falsification window" — same paragraph, single-word swap, fixes the terminology inversion and the c3 Hole 3 carryover in one strike.

text_fingerprint.verdict_chip_target: Refuted by Data (eclipse argument cluster — WIN-010)
text_fingerprint.notes: Coherence-rule paragraph from EXP-275 is in the live section but is OUT OF SCOPE for this queue 284 review. queue 289 (SEC-4.2.2-EXP275) is the dedicated re-review for that integration.

deeper_analysis:
The two ISS-1671/1651 patches are exactly the kind of content the batching gate (Step 8a) would normally allow: both moderate (not minor — so the gate excludes batching anyway), both surgical, no verdict change, no new arguments introduced. The c3 Hole 0 fix (Domain 11 → neutral attribution) is genuinely valuable — it removes a small but easily-exploitable rhetorical opening ("that label doesn’t exist on our page") and replaces it with an attribution that survives the dome author’s frequent layout reorganizations. The c3 Hole 3 fix (3.6× arithmetic) is purely a precision improvement; the original "3.6× outside the envelope" was accurate but loose and could have been re-cited by a defender as a unit error. Showing the division (|−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6) closes that opening.

Advocate Mode — strongest defender comeback against the patched text: a defender now has to attack the BOU-2017 trilemma as a whole rather than picking at the labelling. The strongest remaining defender move is the post-Sq residual escape (paragraph 7) which §4.2.2 already pre-empts by demanding pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology. The defender CAN still note that "acceptance window" inverts the rule semantically — but that’s a self-own (admitting the dome PASSES when the measurement is INSIDE ±3 of 0 means BOU 2017’s −10.9 nT is in the dome-FAILING region, which is the opposite of what the dome wants to establish for 2026). The terminology inversion is therefore a copy-edit issue for us, not a substantive argument for them.

Cross-WIN consistency check: §4.2.2’s ±3 nT framing is consistent with §4.2.3 (hedging structure), §4.2.5 (input chain), and §4.2.7 (geometry independent failure). The two patches do not break any internal anchor links (all three #p4-423, #p4-425, #p4-427 still resolve in part4.html).

Quantitative verification: 10.9 / 3 = 3.6333…, rounds to 3.6 (2 sig figs) — text correct. ±3 nT half-width terminology: technically correct (the half-width of an interval [-X, X] is X), even if non-canonical for magnetometry. No regressions, no fabricated citations introduced.

summary_for_decider: queue 284 (SEC-4.2.2 ISS-1671 + ISS-1651) — both patches landed cleanly, no regressions, no new substantive holes. Recommended action: no_change. Three pre-existing minor polish items (acceptance/falsification terminology + c3 minors 2 and 4) plus one new-minor (nested parenthetical readability) carried over for the operator’s holistic-pass attention; none rise to the bar of fresh issue filing. If you pick up any one, the highest-value is acceptance→falsification window in the BOU-trilemma paragraph (single-word, also closes c3 Hole 3 carryover). Verdict (Refuted by Data) firmly holds. Note: queue 289 (SEC-4.2.2-EXP275 coherence rule) is the dedicated re-review for the EXP-275 paragraph and is independent of this review.
